learning_outcomes:
1 1.  |  Major Programme:
Graduates from the programme will have:
(1)  |  acquired a sound
knowledge of the Eastern and Western (both continental and analytic)
philosophical traditions (K/S);
(2)  |  gained an
understanding of the historical and cultural backgrounds of philosophical
thoughts (K/S);
(3)  |  gained the ability to
read and interpret classical philosophical texts, to analyze the content, and
to evaluate the arguments (K/S);
(4)  |  acquired a grasp of
the central issues of philosophy and to reflect on their contemporary
relevance (K/S/V);
(5)  |  learnt basic concepts
and methods in logic, to assess arguments in various contexts, and to
identify and analyze informal fallacies (K/S);
(6)  |  gained the ability to
engage in critical discussion, to put forward and defend their own ideas, and
to master the expository and critical skills for argumentative writing (K/S);
(7)  |  fostered a
sensibility toward ethical, social, political, and cultural issues (K/S/V);
(8)  |  appreciated the role
and importance of philosophical reflection in various matters and to bring it
to bear on things that matter in life (K/S/V);
(9)  |  become an independent
and critical thinker and to be equipped with life-long learning capacities
(K/S/V).
K = Knowledge outcomes; S = Skills
outcomes; V = Value and attitude outcomes
2.  |  Minor Programme:
Upon the completion of the Minor Programme,
students will have demonstrated basic understanding of fundamental issues
related to the subject, acquired knowledge of some aspects of the subject
with sufficient exposure and at a suitable level to allow further study in
this subject or related subjects or to pursue a related career.
study_scheme:
Philosophy Philosophy Applicable to students admitted in 2025-26
Major Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 63 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
PHIL1110*
and any two courses from ANTH1020,
ARTS1005, 1010, BMBL1001, CHES 1101 or 1102, CHLL1900 or 1902 or 1903, CURE1000
or 1400, 1110, ENGE1000, FAAS1900, HIST1000 or 1700 or 1701, JASP1090,
LING1000, MUSC1000, THEO1000, TRAN1000
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  30
PHIL1110*, 1310, 2020,
2030, 2040, 2050, 2060, 3000, 3800, 3820, 4800 (Capstone course)
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  24
All
Philosophy courses other than PHIL1110 and the 30 units of required
courses listed in (2) are elective courses. Students are required to take
at least 6 units each from Areas 1, 2 and 3. Final year students are
allowed to take a maximum of 6 units of courses from Area 5.
Total:  |  63
* PHIL1110 counted as Faculty
package units only. Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  PHIL courses at 2000 and above level will be
included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students
should obtain Grade “C-” or above in each of the courses of PHIL1110 and
1310. Otherwise, they are required to repeat the course(s). The repeat of
the course(s) will not, however, affect the normal sequence of students’
selection of courses. Students who cannot meet the Grade “C-” requirement
in any one of the courses mentioned above after two attempts will be
required to withdraw from the University.
3.  |  Exemption
of units may be granted to students given permission to take two minor programmes.
The Department will consider each application on a case-by-case basis.

Major Programme Requirement (for
Associate Degree or Higher Diploma holders admitted to senior-year places)
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 54 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  3
PHIL1110*
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  24
PHIL1110*, 1310, 2020, 2030, 2040,
2050, 4800 and two courses from PHIL3000, 3800, 3820
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  27
All Philosophy courses (including
PHIL3000/3800/3820 not taken to fulfill requirement (2) above) on the
course list other than the 27 units of required courses listed in (1) and
(2) are elective courses. Students are required to take at least 3 units
each from Areas 1, 2 and 3. Final year students are allowed to take a
maximum of 6 units of courses from Area 5.
Total:  |  54
* Units of PHIL1110 counted as
Faculty package units only. Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  PHIL courses at 2000 and above level will be
included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students are recommended to take PHIL1110,
1310, 2020, 2030, 2040 and 2050 in their first year of attendance.
3.  |  Students should obtain Grade “C-” or above in each of the
courses of PHIL1110 and 1310. Otherwise, they are required to repeat the
courses. The repeat of these courses will not, however, affect the normal
sequence of students’ selection of courses. Students who cannot meet the
Grade “C-” requirement in any one of the courses mentioned above after
two attempts will be required to withdraw from the University.

Minor Programme Title Philosophy
Minor Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 18 units of courses, with at least
6 units at 3000 or above level, as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Course:  |  3
PHIL1110
2.  |  Elective Courses:  |  15
PHIL courses other than PHIL1110 are
elective courses. Students are required to take at least 6 units of
courses at 3000 or above level.
Total:  |  18
Explanatory
Notes: 1. This Minor Programme is not applicable to
students who major in Philosophy. 2. No more than 9 units of courses taken to
fulfill the requirements of the students’ Major Programme(s) and other Minor
Programme(s) respectively can be used to fulfill the requirement of this Minor Programme.
